reviews.review_msg: Tiffany's classes usually lacked the critical approach I expected for an upper level class. She marked the close reading exercise really hard and the final indeed, was not meant to last 3 hours. On the bright side, her 4 hour classes were not boring. Her frequently used phrase "I don't know" undermined the validity of our answers: how annoying!

I'm not sure what you guys are on about. This professor is worthless, she almost never teaches but relies on group work to suck up class time. She allows complete morons to ramble on. Her assignments are as clear as mud. The final was BRUTAL and she claims that you only need 3 hours to write it. Never again.

Tiffany is an breath of fresh air in a department and subject that occasionally seems at tad stodgy.  Her classes are incredibly well run, with snappy lectures and helpful group work.  I'd take another of her classes in a heart-beat.

I don't think i've had a teacher more helpful than Tiffany has been. She teaches well, looks over your sources when you had in your paper proposal and tells you if they'll be helpful or not, and relates well to her students. I've had her twice and would definately take her again. She's very educated and extremely nice and approachable.

Professor Alkan took a little bit of getting used to. But she is honestly one of the better professors I have had. She is so so so helpful as well, very welcome to discussing different ideas with students.
Very talented at lecturing. Engaging, concise, expressive, and relevant. Highly recommended.
Doesnt write anything down,Extremely boring to listen too, Assignments are terrible. BUT she is very attractive so it makes up for it

Great professor - most likely my favourite of all profs I've had at SFU.  Awesome seminar prof - really really helpful when creating/researching papers and is really interested in the subject matter (early modern romance lit). Highly recommended!

Tiffany seems like a new professor that loves her subject but sitting in class is like being in a episode of dawson's creek she always has to tell us the meaning of her words and the readings she chose are horrible! She is not clear on what is important and she doesn't make the subject interesting. She is very boring to listen to.
